title: eriogonum microtheca subsp. laxiflorum
intro: Eriogonum microtheca subsp. laxiflorum, also known as a shrubby buckwheat, is a species of flowering plant in the family Polygonaceae, native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and astringent properties. The health benefits of Eriogonum microtheca subsp. laxiflorum include its ability to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be taken internally or applied topically to promote healing and soothe irritated skin.
